mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easing financial conditions that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy is moving down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after monthly FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising readings, or if the sign relation reverses in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real rates tighten and valuation pressure rises, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 4% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening labor income and recession risk, which should depress equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment increases are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the conditional hit rate is indistinguishable from 50% across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth in PAYEMS captures improving aggregate labor income and demand, so persistent monthly gains should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month streaks of PAYEMS growth fail to precede positive median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses sign consistency in a recovery-phase sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS and HOUST together indicate broad final demand and housing-linked spending, which should support cyclical earnings and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST are both rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if joint monthly rises in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform single-series conditions, or if the combined trigger yields negative median 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals industrial contraction and weaker corporate output, which should reduce earnings expectations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly drops in INDPRO are not associated with weaker 20-day SPX returns than rising or flat industrial production, or if the effect disappears after revision-adjustment checks.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST after a housing slump signals construction recovery and a rebound in demand-sensitive activity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% month over month after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebounds do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips negative in post-trough samples.

mechanism_narrative: During a late tightening cycle, rising FEDFUNDS combined with flat or falling CPIAUCSL increases the odds that policy is overtightening relative to inflation, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is rising for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is below 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined tightening-plus-low-inflation condition does not predict negative median 60-day SPX returns, or if positive outcomes exceed 60% of triggered cases.
